title: Smoky Russian Lamb Broth
description: A hearty, smoky broth featuring lamb, vegetables, and a variety of aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat the lamb neck bones in a large Dutch oven over medium heat, until browned on all sides.
Add the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the bay leaves, black peppercorns, smoked paprika, and thyme to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the beef broth and water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids.
Stir in the lemon juice and season the broth with salt to taste.
Return the pot to the stovetop and bring the broth to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the broth has reduced slightly.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
